title: Lot Alteration, CRB Reappointment, and Axon Contract Legal Preparation
summary: Three items: (1) Deputy Commissioner of Code Enforcement presents lot alteration at 153-159 to straighten diagonal property line between occupied house and adjacent Greater Syracuse Land Bank lot for R3 zone compliance, eliminating split-zone and existing fence encroachment; (2) Resolution to reappoint Linda Bats to Citizen Review Board — council notes multiple CRB vacancies from both council and mayoral appointments are overdue per legislation; (3) First Assistant Corporation Counsel announces Law Department and SPD prepared to discuss Axon contract legal aspects in executive session.
raw_content:

[00:33:50] [clears throat] explain this is the two

speakers.name: Jake Dishaw
speakers.role: Deputy Commissioner of Code Enforcement and Zoning

political_signal.description: Council highlights unfilled CRB appointments from both council and mayor beyond legislative deadlines; Axon contract legal review signals careful oversight

processing_notes: Axon contract discussion moved to executive session — substantive legal aspects not publicly recorded
CRB legislation allows council, mayor, or CRB itself to appoint when vacancies are overdue
Lot alteration policy: avoid split-zone parcels on resub or lot alteration
Axon discussion connects to Feb 6 Joint Public Safety committee meeting (b75K4ejDfY4)
Segment 19 of 19 from Common Council Study Session, January 21st 2026
